data.narrative:
OneBrief, a Series B defense and security company founded by Remi Cossart, has raised $47M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms across the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $47M total raised as a proxy for current value, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $89M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables—Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns—with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B. The median across this set is $1.2B. The two methods produce meaningfully different outputs, reflecting the wide dispersion in late-stage defense-tech valuations.

The triangulated range spans $76M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $645M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables drive the upper bound. Key uncertainties include whether OneBrief's growth trajectory and customer traction align with higher-valued peers or represent a more modest Series B profile, and whether recent public market conditions for defense-tech have compressed multiples relative to the private peer set. Further diligence on revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and technology differentiation would narrow this range materially.
